channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Bayelsa community laments collapse of 27-year hospital
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Okoroba community in Bayelsa laments the collapse of its 27-year-old Cottage Hospital, appealing to the government for rehabilitation and staff reposting.
Read More: https://punchng.com/bayelsa-community-laments-collapse-of-27-year-hospital/

item.title: Delta Assembly begins recess, Speaker urges constituency visits
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Delta State House of Assembly has begun its six-week annual recess. Speaker Emomotimi Guwor urged members to visit constituencies and attend training d
Read More: https://punchng.com/delta-assembly-begins-recess-speaker-urges-constituency-visits/

item.title: Otu promises funding, employment for presidential awardees
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Governor Bassey Otu pledges funding, employment, and support for Cross River youths who received the Presidential Honours Award for their exceptional NYSC
Read More: https://punchng.com/otu-promises-funding-employment-for-presidential-awardees/

item.title: C’River trains 221 for social intervention
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cross River State trains 221 facilitators for a social intervention programme aimed at supporting vulnerable residents with cash transfers and empowerment
Read More: https://punchng.com/criver-trains-221-for-social-intervention/

item.title: Cleric urges parties to avoid same-faith tickets in Kaduna, Plateau
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A cleric advises political parties to avoid same-faith tickets in Kaduna and Plateau for 2027 elections, promoting unity and inclusivity in diverse states.
Read More: https://punchng.com/cleric-urges-parties-to-avoid-same-faith-tickets-in-kaduna-plateau/

item.title: Security forces kill three bandits, rescue businessman in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kwara security forces neutralised three bandits and rescued a kidnapped businessman in Kaiama LGA. Learn how the joint operation foiled the attack.
Read More: https://punchng.com/security-forces-kill-three-bandits-rescue-businessman-in-kwara/

item.title: Tinubu jittery over Makinde’s growing popularity – APM
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
APM claims President Tinubu is “jittery” over Governor Seyi Makinde’s growing popularity ahead of the 2027 presidential election, citing federal government

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos Anglican Bishop, Rt Revd Dr Ebenezer Adewole, urges Nigerian leaders to embrace accountability and integrity for national transformation.

item.title: Osun 2026: Oyebamiji warns against voter harassment
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
APC candidate Bola Oyebamiji assures Osun residents of a peaceful 2026 election, warning against voter harassment and urging participation without fear.
Read More: https://punchng.com/osun-2026-oyebamiji-warns-against-voter-harassment/

item.title: Arsenal Rule Out Surgery For Injured Saliba
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Arsenal have confirmed that William Saliba will not undergo surgery for his back injury but will be out for an extended period. Saliba was substituted in the 30th minute of France’s World Cup semi-final defeat to Spain. “William Saliba has returned to us from World Cup duty, where he pla

item.title: Milan, Fiorentina Bologna Target Chukwueze as He Faces Uncertain Future at Milan
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Following uncertainty over his future with AC Milan, rival Italian clubs, Fiorentina and Bologna have taken bold steps to sign Super Eagles winger, Samuel Chukwueze from the Rossoneri this summer.

item.title: Nasdaq to Kick Off Tiny Companies Faster After SEC Approves Rule
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Nasdaq Inc. will change its rules to make it easier to kick off flailing businesses as regulators zero in on wild market swings and allegations of manipulation that have plagued trading in so-called microcap stocks.

item.description: THE story of Lagos’ budget performance over the past year is a story of planning, resilience and strategic investment. item.title: Finch: England's handling of Stokes and McCullum exits has been a circus
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Former Australia batter Aaron Finch likened England's recent coaching and captaincy upheaval to a 'circus' and says many Australians have been watching things unfold from afar 'giggling' at their old rivals.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as Dip-Buying Counters Inflation Fears From War
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold held gains on dip-buying even as hostilities in the Middle East pushed oil prices to multiweek highs, rekindling concerns that US inflationary pressure could prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

item.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

item.description: Derek McInnes insists Rangers are right to contest the four-match touchline ban he received for describing as "disgusting" the decision to award Celtic a late penalty at Motherwell in May.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A record pile of yuan in Hong Kong is making its way to corporate loans while driving a surge in capital market activity, signaling that China’s drive to internationalize its currency is advancing.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Credit to Nigeria’s private sector increased by 9.37 per cent, or N7.13 trillion, to N83.26 trillion in June 2026, compared with N76.13 trillion, in the corresponding period of last year, latest data from the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) shows. item.title: CAF expands AFCON to 28 teams
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) will feature 28 teams instead of 24 starting from the 2027 tournament, according to Confederation of African Football (CAF) President Patrice Motsepe.

item.title: Ukrainians welcome new army chief but seek return of fired defence minister
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Ukraine's soldiers and civilians welcomed the appointment of Mykhailo Drapaty as the new army chief on Wednesday, but many said they still wanted former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov restored after his surprise removal sparked the largest war-related protests since Russia's 2022 invasion.

item.title: US court sets June 2027 trial for Venezuela’s ousted Maduro
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Venezuela's ousted president Nicolas Maduro and his wife will go on trial in the United States in June 2027, a court ruled Wednesday, after the US military captured them in a stunning nighttime raid in January. item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US equity-index futures declined after Alphabet Inc.’s spending plans intensified concerns about the payoff from the industry’s aggressive artificial intelligence buildout. Oil extended its rally.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to NAFDAC, the nationwide enforcement operation is holding in the six geopolitical zones and follows the closure of manufacturing facilities found to have violated the ban.

item.description: Wall Street banks have started buying and selling an initial piece of a $35 billion financing package for Broadcom Inc. and Anthropic PBC’s AI infrastructure expansion, with the biggest private credit deal ever now being offered to a wider pool of investors.

item.title: France Records Nearly 5,800 Excess Deaths During Record Heatwave
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: French health authorities say a record-breaking heatwave last month led to 5,764 more deaths than expected, describing the excess mortality among people aged 15 and older as "unprecedented." item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Trump administration says 'historic' agreement will 'reinforce global nonproliferation standards' and create jobs in US.

item.title: Salt stars as Fire end eight-match Hundred losing streak against Brave
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Phil Salt weathered a bowling storm from England fast bowler Jofra Archer as the Welsh Fire captain led his side to a six-wicket victory over Southern Brave in The Hundred.

item.title: Growing Smartphone Use Heightens Cyber Risks, Kaspersky Warns
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Cybersecurity firm Kaspersky has urged smartphone users to adopt stronger security measures, warning that while mobile phones have become the primary gateway to the internet for millions of people, users’ security practices have failed to keep pace with the growing volume of sensitive informat

item.title: What wealthy Nigerians should know about the U.S. EB-5 Green Card programme
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: For wealthy Nigerians looking to relocate to the United States, one immigration pathway that often attracts attention is the EB-5 Immigrant Investor Program. The post What wealthy Nigerians should know about the U.S. EB-5 Green Card programme appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Trump threatens Iranian infrastructure over Strait of Hormuz attacks
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump warned on Wednesday that the US would destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant every time Tehran attacked shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The threat came as Washington and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, regional missile alerts sounded and diplomatic efforts remained stalle

item.title: PHOTOS: Wike’s daughter bags law degree from UK varsity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Jazmyne Wike, the daughter of FCT Minister, Nyesom Wike has graduated with a Bachelor of Laws degree from University of Southampton, United Kingdom. The post PHOTOS: Wike’s daughter bags law degree from UK varsity appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Food prices, Middle East tensions drive CBN’s latest decision
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has decided to keep its main interest rate, known as the Monetary Policy Rate (MPR), at 26.5 per cent.
